subject: ap-calculus-ab
title: Particle Motion
stimulus: Rocket A has positive velocity $v(t)$ after being launched upward from an initial height of 0 at time $t = 0$. The velocity of the rocket is recorded for selected values of $t$ over the interval $0 \le t \le 80$ seconds, as shown in the table above.

parts.prompt: Find the average acceleration of rocket A over the time interval $0 \le t \le 80$ seconds. Indicate units of measure.
rubric_criteria: Find the average acceleration of rocket A over the time interval $0 \le t \le 80$ seconds. Indicate units of measure.

parts.prompt: Using correct units, explain the meaning of $\int_{10}^{70} v(t)\, dt$ in terms of the rocket's flight. Use a midpoint Riemann sum with 3 subintervals of equal length to approximate $\int_{10}^{70} v(t)\, dt$.
rubric_criteria: Using correct units, explain the meaning of $\int_{10}^{70} v(t)\, dt$ in terms of the rocket's flight. Use a midpoint Riemann sum with 3 subintervals of equal length to approximate $\int_{10}^{70} v(t)\, dt$.

parts.prompt: Rocket B is launched upward with an acceleration of $a(t) = \dfrac{3}{\sqrt{t+1}}$ feet per second per second. At time $t = 0$ seconds, the initial height of the rocket is 0 feet, and the initial velocity is 2 feet per second. Which of the two rockets is traveling faster at time $t = 80$ seconds? Explain your answer.
rubric_criteria: Rocket B is launched upward with an acceleration of $a(t) = \dfrac{3}{\sqrt{t+1}}$ feet per second per second. At time $t = 0$ seconds, the initial height of the rocket is 0 feet, and the initial velocity is 2 feet per second. Which of the two rockets is traveling faster at time $t = 80$ seconds? Explain your answer.
